freeTools.title: Free image helpers
freeTools.lead: Upscale, beauty, cutout, and cleanup — fast CV / restoration stacks, not chat LLMs.

auth.oauthHint: For OAuth, configure redirect URLs in Google Cloud / Azure as

axios.htmlError: The API returned HTML instead of JSON (common causes: ① FastAPI not running or wrong port ② set VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET in frontend/.env.development ③ configure /api reverse proxy in production). HTTP {status}

profile.title: My page
profile.needLoginTitle: Sign in to see your profile
profile.needLoginHint: After signing in, your creations and VIP status will sync across devices.

packsPage.title: Photo packs
packsPage.intro: Each chip opens a curated style grid for that theme. Below, packs are sorted under the same scene lanes as the homepage.
packsPage.empty: No packs are available right now. Please try again later.

toolsPage.title: Free tools
toolsPage.subtitle: Lightweight editors you can ship without a conversational LLM — classic inpainting, super-resolution, segmentation, and swap pipelines.
toolsPage.disclaimer: Roadmap descriptions only: wiring to GPU workers, consent checks, and rate limits still belong in product and legal review before launch.

objectRemover.title: Object remover
objectRemover.pain: Landmarks packed with tourists, or an ex in an old couple shot — users want them gone in one gesture.
objectRemover.stack: Stable stack: coarse brush mask + LaMa-style inpainting (often packaged via IOPaint).
objectRemover.product: Keep it foolproof: scribble on what to remove, release, and show an instant patch preview.

restoreUpscale.title: Rescue blurry shots
restoreUpscale.pain: Night selfies, compressed chat saves, and aged camera rolls that deserve a second life.
restoreUpscale.stack: Real-ESRGAN for global upscale/detail recovery, CodeFormer when faces need believable reconstruction.
restoreUpscale.product: Market as “One-tap 4K glow” or “lossless enlargement for social saves” with a single preset slider.

beauty.title: Beauty & skin polish
beauty.pain: Users want brighter skin and cleaner texture without launching a full creative suite.
beauty.stack: Guided filters, bilateral / guided smoothing, tone curves — all CPU-friendly when tuned.
beauty.product: Offer subtle “studio skin” presets plus intensity guardrails so faces stay recognizable.

bgRemove.title: Background remover
bgRemove.pain: Stickers, memes, and collage layers — not poster design — are the real C-end use case.
bgRemove.stack: rembg on top of U²-Net (or similar) for crisp alpha mattes in a couple of seconds on CPU.
bgRemove.product: Prioritize speed and a obvious “Download transparent PNG” button after preview.

faceSwap.title: Face swap (high risk)
faceSwap.pain: Viral jokes and mashups drive shares, but misuse scales just as fast.
faceSwap.stack: FaceFusion / roop-class pipelines behind an API with strict logging.
faceSwap.product: Treat as compliance-heavy: block NSFW targets, require clear consent, watermark outputs, and throttle free tier.

pricingPage.title: Pricing & credits
pricingPage.intro: Generation uses credits. The rules below match the live API (resolution tier, optional background removal, regenerate discount, signup bonus).
pricingPage.creditsLead: The picker labels 512 / 1024 / 2048 / 4096 are the output size tiers; each successful task deducts the listed credits.
pricingPage.addonBg: If you enable background removal on a new generation, +2 credits are added to the resolution cost before deduction.
pricingPage.regenerate: When you regenerate from an existing task (same flow, with regenerate flag), the service charges 50% of the full price (resolution + background add-on if applicable), rounded up to the next whole credit. The breakdown is stored on the task for auditing.
pricingPage.signupBody: New accounts receive {credits} bonus credits once (first login / registration). The grant is recorded in your credit ledger and is not repeated.
pricingPage.subscriptionBody: Paid credit packs and subscription renewals are intended to top up your balance through checkout (e.g. Stripe) and webhooks. Exact SKUs, monthly credit amounts, and auto-renew terms are defined at purchase time on the checkout page and in your order confirmation — not on this static summary.
pricingPage.linksLead: Your operator configures these URLs in Admin → Payments ("Pricing page · subscription URL" / "credit pack URL"). Links open in a new tab.
pricingPage.linksEmpty: No subscription or credit-pack URL is configured yet. Add HTTPS links in the admin payment settings to show buttons here.
pricingPage.ledgerBody: Your remaining credits and every grant or consumption appear in the account ledger (kinds such as signup_bonus, consume_generate, and future purchase_pack / subscription_refill). The server enforces balance before each task; insufficient credits return HTTP 402.
pricingPage.checkoutNote: If checkout or subscription products are not configured yet, you may still use bonus or admin-granted credits. This page reflects the technical rules implemented in the API today.
